settings.title: Tooth-Coloured Fillings
settings.summary: Quiet repairs for decay or small chips that blend in with the rest of your smile.
settings.description:
When a tooth has a cavity or a minor crack, a filling stops it spreading and restores the bite. Catching these early keeps the repair small and the tooth strong, which is one more reason regular check-ups are worth it.

What the appointment involves

We use composite material matched to your natural shade, so the repair is hard to spot. The appointment is straightforward, and we numb the area first so you stay comfortable throughout. We remove the decay or tidy the chip, then build up the tooth in layers and shape it to fit how you bite.

  • Gentle numbing so the area stays comfortable
  • A shade matched to your surrounding teeth
  • The bite checked and adjusted before you leave

Most fillings are done in a single visit. The composite sets straight away, so you can usually eat and drink as normal once any numbness wears off.

Looking after the repair

Tooth-coloured fillings hold up well with everyday care. Brushing twice a day and keeping up with cleans helps them last, and we will check them at routine visits to make sure the edges stay sound.

If you ever notice the bite feels high or the tooth becomes sensitive, let us know. A quick adjustment is simple to make, and we would rather sort it early than have you put up with it.
